key_insight: Substrate lattice matching controls whether confined deuterium crystallizes as smooth coherent epitaxy or defect-rich polycrystals.
engineering_relevance: For ICF cryogenic targets, tuning the inner-surface lattice to the deuterium equilibrium spacing is a key design rule for producing high-integrity, ultra-smooth fuel layers.
method: Large-scale molecular dynamics of D2 in spherical ablator shells
Feynman-Hibbs corrected Silvera-Goldman potential to include low-temperature quantum effects
Systematic sweep of substrate lattice constant from 3.1 to 3.9 Å
evidence_signal: The simulations show a clear transition near the ~3.5 Å match point from layer-by-layer HCP-dominated growth to island-like, polycrystalline growth with higher roughness and defects.
watch_item: The results are model-based and depend on the chosen potential and idealized spherical shell geometry, so experimental validation is needed for real DT targets.
paper_title: Lattice Matching Dictates the Growth Mode and Quality of Deuterium Crystallization in Confined Spherical Shells
